SELECT event_date, timezone(event_date) AS tz_offset FROM events WHERE event_date < DATE '2000-01-01';
CREATE TABLE locations (location_id INT, location_name VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO locations VALUES (1, 'New York'), (2, 'Los Angeles');
SELECT l.location_name, timezone(e.event_date) FROM events e JOIN locations l ON l.location_id = 1;
WITH timezones AS (SELECT event_date, timezone(event_date) AS tz FROM events) SELECT * FROM timezones WHERE tz IS NOT NULL;
SELECT e.event_date, l.location_name, timezone(e.event_date) FROM events e JOIN locations l ON l.location_id IN (1, 2) WHERE e.event_date > DATE '1990-01-01';
CREATE TABLE user_events (user_id INT, event_date DATE);
INSERT INTO user_events VALUES (1, DATE '1992-02-15'), (2, DATE '1995-12-25');
SELECT ue.user_id, ue.event_date, timezone(ue.event_date) FROM user_events ue WHERE ue.event_date IN (SELECT event_date FROM events);
SELECT COUNT(*) AS event_count, timezone(event_date) FROM events GROUP BY timezone(event_date);

SELECT l.location_name, COUNT(e.event_date) AS number_of_events, timezone(e.event_date) FROM events e JOIN locations l ON e.event_date < DATE '2020-01-01' GROUP BY l.location_name, timezone(e.event_date);
SELECT timezone(DATE '2023-01-01' + INTERVAL '1 day'), timezone(DATE '2023-01-01' - INTERVAL '1 month');
